reasoning: definite article before nouns such as sense, world, rules, second step, and parts in all shown contexts
preposition linking noun phrases in intrinsic value of a whole, sufferings of others, pursuit of truth, and Ethics of Plato
most shown uses introduce infinitives such as to establish, to discover, to content, and to be thought; one context has prepositional to this class
shown contexts are mainly copular uses such as pleasure is good, knowledge is good, is general, and it is plain
several shown uses introduce complement clauses, as in difficulty that is found, established that good is good, and rule that all great goods; one use is demonstrative in this is a chair
personal or expletive pronoun in all contexts, including it is absurd, it certainly has, what it commands, and you preferred it
preposition in locative or abstract prepositional phrases such as in this case, in another place, and in its practical conclusions
indefinite article before nouns such as marked distinction, part, proof, cognition, and minute
shown uses include auxiliary or infinitival be in may be made, remains to be done, is to be good means, and are always to be done; some copular uses are also present
coordinating conjunction joining words, phrases, and clauses in all shown contexts
mostly relative pronoun introducing clauses, as in actions to which, those which include, and questions which philosophers answer
first-person plural pronoun in all contexts, including we leave, we should be entitled, we had first been struck, and we shall deal
primarily predicate or attributive adjective in good is good, things are good, good produced, and good conduct/value contexts, though Moore also treats it as a concept
several contexts use as to introduce clauses or comparisons, such as since as above stated and as if; others are prepositional in as instances and as means
often demonstrative determiner before nouns such as preference, something, and standard; one context has standalone this in denote this and only this
negative particle in contexts such as not another thing, not maintain, and are not included
mostly copular uses such as generic qualities are, principles are, goods are numerous, and virtues are dispositions
interrogative or relative pronoun standing for a proposition or thing in what it does mean, what is productive, what is real, and what exists
coordinating conjunction marking alternatives in evil or ugly, will or otherwise, pleasure or consciousness, and other paired alternatives
preposition introducing agent, means, or relation in biased by, made by Spinoza, bound by laws, and distinguished by
shown uses often form perfect or semi-auxiliary constructions such as have tried, have concluded, have led, and have to consider, though some lexical possession uses occur
first-person singular pronoun in all displayed contexts, including I have endeavoured, I am asked, I am maintaining, and I desire
coordinating conjunction contrasting clauses or phrases in all shown contexts
modal auxiliary expressing possibility or permission in we may urge, it may be observed, I may point out, and may be able
shown uses are prepositional purpose or relation uses, such as for knowledge, for its own sake, for the same reason, and for preferring
subordinating conjunction introducing conditional clauses in all displayed contexts
preposition introducing complements such as with later, with the Metaphysics, with regard to, and with these
third-person plural pronoun referring to propositions, philosophers, laws, or assumed agents in all contexts
focus adverb restricting scope in it only states, these two questions and these only, not only, and can only be
common noun naming the ethical object or state in consciousness of pleasure, gaining pleasure, and pleasure and absence of pain
common abstract noun in contexts such as intrinsic value, value of, degree of value, and owes its value
indefinite article before nouns such as end, action, established rule, and enquiry
determiner before nouns such as value, clear separation, one, which, and guidance in the shown contexts
several displayed uses are lexical possession or attribution, as in has a meaning, object has been identified, and Evolution has little to say; one perfect has followed is auxiliary
modal auxiliary in can be defined, can never be, how can he distinguish, and Ethics can ask
mostly determiner before plural or mass nouns such as these elements, actions, great goods, and all the ordinary objects; some standalone uses occur
often numeral or quantifier contrasting one with another or one of the ends; some generic-pronoun uses may occur elsewhere
most contexts are modal future auxiliaries such as the end will not justify, argument will go, and action will generally be followed; one context discusses Will as a noun
third-person singular pronoun referring to Sidgwick, Mill, Spencer, or another male author in all shown contexts
shown uses mostly degree or anaphoric adverb, as in so much good, all so anxious, it is so, and if all this be so
adjectival modifier in other kind, other parts, other properties, other moralist, and other grounds
preposition marking source or separation in from the fact, different from, distinguishes it from cognition, and follow from this supposition
negative determiner before nouns such as objection, definition, more than a probability, part, and dutiful action
modal auxiliary expressing conditionality in would produce, would be diminished, would be false, and would have value
possessive determiner before nouns such as metaphysical form, realisation, own sake, utility, and rule
determiner before nouns in such actions, no such unique object, such distinction, and such a metaphysical principle; one such source also quantifies a noun phrase
modal auxiliary of necessity in must be conclusive, must form, must be truly good, must rest, and must know
several contexts use the verb meaning denotes, as in it means, this means, Spencer means, and good means good; one context has noun phrase by means of
common noun referring to an object or entity in such a thing, natural good thing, certain thing, and a thing be good for me
common abstract noun in contexts such as this question, answer to this question, our question, distinct question, and the question may mean
used as a substantive philosophical noun in the whole, a whole, this whole, and on the whole
plural common noun referring to entities or objects in all contexts, including other things, living things, and things belonging to classes
names the discipline or field in Metaphysical Ethics, Ethics exists, and Ethics has to recognise; capitalization reflects title/field usage rather than a person or place
preposition in phrases such as on the whole, on condition, on the side of, and fed on one another
mostly demonstrative determiner before nouns such as wholes, properties, conceptions, errors, and may; one context has standalone to these
comparative marker after rather, bearing, worse, and better-quality comparisons in all shown contexts
shown uses are mostly auxiliary or emphatic do in do think, do exist, do not refuse, and do mean; do so is the main competing pro-verb use
preposition in at most, aim at, at some length, at least, and at all events
comparative degree modifier in more or less perfect, far more desirable, no more than, any more difficult, and more evolved
predicate adjective in be true, is true, true for one, and true that life is pleasant
common noun naming the object of cognition, aesthetic appreciation, affection, or desire in all displayed contexts
determiner before nouns such as time, being, intrinsic value, supersensible property, degree, and specific emotion
possessive determiner before nouns such as belief, result, actions, own happiness, and first class
reflexive pronoun referring back to the good thing, word, proposition, or consciousness in all contexts
modal auxiliary of obligation or expectation in doctrine should fulfil, Ethics should be based, should be noticed, and we should not
modifies nouns such as kind, animal, characters, way, and things; it functions like an indefinite determiner in some contexts but is adjectival in the displayed phrases
common abstract noun in the fact that, this fact, in fact, and aware of this fact
auxiliary do in questions and negation, including does Mill attempt, does not ask, does draw attention, and does not suggest
shown contexts are mostly existential there is/there may/there are, but the project tag guide lists here and there under ADV; tagging as adverbial existential
past participle auxiliary in has been explained, has been laid, has ever been given, has been caused, and had not been understood
adjective modifying philosophers, theory, truths, investigations, and related nouns in all contexts
discourse or temporal adverb in would then include, then the fact, would then be, and much more then
adjective modifying action, pleasure, justification, and things, or used predicatively in to be possible
subordinating conjunction introducing temporal or conditional clauses in when used, when we assert, when I say, and when they say
additive adverb in but also, also involve, itself be also good, should also appreciate, and have also pointed out
possessive determiner before nouns such as own happiness, case, practical principles, Intuitionism, and argument
first-person plural object pronoun in allow us, agree with us, bring us pleasure, entitles us, and tells us
focus adverb in even if, even in the isolated case, take even the most favourable, not even necessary, and even if he be not
cardinal number modifying reasons, questions, alternatives, natural objects, and propositions
main verb of cognition in what you think, can we think otherwise, I venture to think, and I think there can be no doubt
common noun in every case, the case of courage, in which case, and in any case
third-person plural object pronoun referring back to alternatives, rules, virtues, propositions, or motives
superlative degree modifier before adjectives such as important, prominent, valuable, and familiar in the shown contexts
displayed contexts split between copular was a part, world was mad, pleasure was the good, and passive auxiliary was kept/was willed; copular uses slightly dominate here
common abstract noun in an action, the action in question, utility of an action, and kind of action
common noun denoting component or portion in essential part, no part, greater part, and part of a whole
shown uses include determiner before actions and instances, but also pronoun-like those who, those which, and some of those; determiner uses are slightly more direct in the sample
main verb meaning denote or intend in may mean, does mean, mean just yellow, we mean to assert, and desirable does mean
modifies nouns or substantives in the same thing, same pleasure, same good, and same kind; the guide also allows same as determiner-like
some contexts are degree adverb uses such as very largely and very complex, while others are intensifying adjective-like uses in the very words, reason, and definition
common abstract noun for goal or purpose in as an end, ultimate rational end, possible end, and universal end
negative modal auxiliary in cannot be denied, cannot itself be identical, cannot be taken, cannot vouch, and cannot possibly do
degree or extent adverb in far greater, far less, so far as, far too many, and in so far as
abstract noun naming being or reality in infer the existence, existence of consciousness, owing to its existence, and mere existence
common abstract noun in good reason, kind of reason, reason of the same thing, and reason why
common abstract noun meaning meaning or respect in this sense, in the sense that, Common Sense, and in the sense that they would not
main linking verb of appearance in seems to be, seems also to owe, seems absolutely necessary, and seems to lie
main verb of utterance or formulation in when we say, nothing better to say, that is to say, and cannot say
possessive determiner before nouns such as existence, answer, own sakes, profession, and objects
possessive determiner before nouns such as business, test, ethical method, own good, and first class
tokenized possessive clitic in Mill's, another's, Sidgwick's, and pleasure's in the shown contexts
discourse adverb marking inference in how therefore, admits therefore, and we must therefore assume
adjective modifying value in all displayed contexts
plural common noun in such actions, expedient actions, kinds of actions, and class of actions
frequency adverb modifying assumes, necessarily done, connected, and among the causes
main verb of existence in to exist, must exist, will exist together, should exist, and ought to exist
adjective modifying difference, part, value, and differences in the displayed contexts
mostly ordinal adjective in first step, first place, and his first appeal; one context has adverbial must first be shewn
temporal or discourse adverb in now forms, may now proceed, shall now see, and have now to face
sample mixes correlative both...and, standalone with both, formed of both, and determiner both classes; tagged as determiner per guide but contexts include pronoun-like uses
used before nouns in another doctrine and another thing, but also nominally in one colour to another and one pleasure than another
adverb modifying written, held, recognised, and paid in the shown contexts
adjective in extremely different, different relations, different with good, and different properties
three contexts use the fixed prepositional phrase with regard to; one context has verbal we regard them
comparative adjective modifying result or used predicatively in is better and generally better as means
indefinite pronoun standing for an unspecified thing or good in something to be aimed at, say something, bring something into existence, and something else
adjective in beautiful object, objects are beautiful, whatever is beautiful, and beautiful qualities
adjective in necessary to be considered, necessary condition, so far as necessary, and the necessary as such

subordinating conjunction introducing alternatives or embedded questions in whether affecting, whether it is not superior, see whether, and whether it be real or not

common noun in imperfect state, state of a man, state of things, and present state of society

common noun in single kind, what kind of, certain kind of authority, and unique in kind
